title: Help: Fix UNOPTFLAT in a case statement?

features.name: always_comb case-based byte rotation
features.description: Code should include an always_comb block with a case statement that permutes byte lanes based on a 2-bit offset derived from address bits, creating a circular dependency flagged as UNOPTFLAT

features.name: combinational case statement with multiple sequential assignments
features.description: Code should include an always_comb block where a case statement assigns multiple signals (a0, a1, a2, a3) based on an offset derived from address bits
features.snippet:
logic [31:0] a0, a1, a2, a3;
logic [1:0] offset;
always_comb begin
  case(offset)
    2'd0: begin a0 = a_by_4; a1 = a_by_4; a2 = a_by_4; a3 = a_by_4; end
    2'd1: begin a0 = a_plus_1; a1 = a_by_4; a2 = a_by_4; a3 = a_by_4; end
    2'd2: begin a0 = a_plus_1; a1 = a_plus_1; a2 = a_by_4; a3 = a_by_4; end
    2'd3: begin a0 = a_plus_1; a1 = a_plus_1; a2 = a_plus_1; a3 = a_by_4; end
  endcase
end
